revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]][[fa:آشیهارا نو ناکاتسوکونی]][[fr:Ashihara no nakatsukuni]][[it:Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]][[ja:葦原中国]][[ko:위원중국]][[pl:Kraj Pośród Uprawnych Pól]][[ru:Асихара-но Накацукуни]][[th:อาชิฮาระ โนะ นากัตสึกูนิ]][[zh:葦原中國]]


{{Unreferenced|date=September 2021|qq=}}{{short description|Japanese mythological place|qq=}}
{{nihongo|'''Ashihara no Nakatsukuni'''|葦原の中つ国|{{trans.|qq=}} ''The middle country of {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|qid=Q620424|lt=Reed bed|1=Reed bed}}s''|qq=}} is, in [[Japanese mythology]], the world between [[Takamagahara]] ({{ill|Heaven|en|Heaven|qid=Q4489450|lt=Heaven|1=Heaven}}) and {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|qid=Q1142558|lt=Yomi|1=Yomi}} ({{ill|Hell|en|Hell|qid=Q564|lt=Hell|1=Hell}}). In time, the term became another word for the country or the location of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The term can be used interchangeably with '''[[Names of Japan#Classical|Toyoashihara no Nakatsukuni]]''' (豊葦原中国). There is a great dispute among historians about where exactly in Japan the term originally referred to.{{Citation needed|date=July 2009|qq=}}
Perhaps the term was considered appropriate to describe Japan because the land was damp and covered with {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|qid=Q620424|lt=reeds|1=Reed bed}} (葦) in ancient times. The meaning of 中 (middle) in the word 中つ国 is based upon the world view of ancient peoples, where 中つ国 indicates the real world or country between [[Takamagahara]] in the heavens and {{ill|Yomi|en|Yomi|qid=Q1142558|lt=Yomi no kuni|1=Yomi}} in the netherworld.  
== References ==
{{reflist|qq=}}
